submission.title: What to do in k35?
submission.body: Hello everyone, I just arrived at k35, thanks to the return event I was able to reach my academy and meeting point at 35, now here I am stuck I think because of so much information that exists, I would like to be able to start doing pvp, I was going to upload my troop buildings To obtain t14, I still don't know if it is convenient, I will publish my troops are few, any help, comments or criticism are accepted to improve my game.

comments.body: Where is your t14.  With 8k t13 ground I am sure you didn’t got your wing yet. Those small coiners try to get a defend keep before wing are stupid
For the same reason I am asking if it is worth going up to get t14 or training t13 and t12

It's hard to know what to do.  I was much like you and decided to train T1 I'm still keep 35.    I wasn't sure what direction I wanted to go so decided I'd go T1 and went all in and only concentrate my RSS gems and speedups on what matters for my defense.  I have zero t14s as of yet but my focus is only on defense.  You need to figure what best suits you.  In under a year I've built a nice defense and still under a billion power.
I’d say shoot for 200m t1 cav 50m t1 ground and 50m t1 ranged. And focus on flat refines on wall.
Either make a t1 wall, get t14s or go k36. I think any of these is reasonable
What are flat refines?  I see that phrase used but don’t know what it means
Yes, there is so much information and so many things to see that I get lost and don't know where to start, thank you
I think I would also like to focus on defense.
I think flat refines are the numerical values ​​and not the percentages.
Thank you, I'll start with that.

Build the T14, and go to k36 because you won’t stand a chance defending without t14 against anyone over a k35. They will have four more layers than you and you’ll just give them a lot of points. You’d be better off staying with a lower troop amount and building t14 so you can ghost imo.

Not a k35, but alliance leader is - he complains about resources and speedups a lot since he is training full t14 marches so I'd recommend prioritising upgrading the academy for the 6th March slot which will come in the most useful in battlefield/gathering etc.

Edit: PVP takes a LOT of troops so would not recommend trying to get to t14 before academy 35
I already have the 6th march.

That is also my doubt, I built from t2 to t10 100k for the defense layers but from t11 onwards I don't know to what extent to do because in the same way as you say many troops I will not be able to make ghost.

Are you only trying to be a defense only keep? If not you will need enough range and ground to build a proper pvp march but if ghost you high tier troops then you can give less points in a defense. Only keep enough to have a layer. I think with 100k layers you are beyond ghosting all your troops
Ok nice - like I said I'm not k35 so no idea what to do outside of that, but good luck!

I got my wings at K35 and maybe 600m power, still able to fully ghost all my troops.

Just depends on the type of player a person is.

Kinda absurd for you to think he doesn’t have a chance to get wings with only 8k t13 ground. I don’t have any t13 ground at all. I lost fights but won the power exchange in SvS. Only saying this cause it’s possible.

https://preview.redd.it/0z55h7zxktud1.jpeg?width=1290&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=63ed834002728b923baa9532e72bd0452e590dc0
I think I had only t11 ground until k38 and i got wings our first svs...no point without a ground general and you're ghosting
You misunderstand it.  I said he only got 8k t13 ground means he didn’t got ground march.  I got my wing when I was k30 with 70m keep power. Only one archer march with 500 ground each layer
Depend on the server.  I can get top 5 easy before server merge.  Now I can’t get top 5 with 3b points. Build a t1 trap defend keep before wonder one really not smart

Now I am k35 with 400m keep power.  Ground defend buff attack 1450%. Defend 1330% Hp 1260%. got 2m ground total with 250k t14 ground.  I can defend most k37 and under archer attack. And 3m under t11 siege bomb

Nice. Yes. I totally misunderstood. I def don’t have a ground march myself. I first started with the plan to get a good enough cav build to hunt the big monsters. Then talked to some guys about doing PvP and train some range. Now I want to do a siege PVP build.

T1 keeps depend too much on what other people do , fortunately for you there's alot of stupid people around , unfortunately for you though you'll face k42+ keeps at some point that spend much more than you and you'll get slaughtered

K40, 7bn, 200m tech power here.

Guys, with the state of the game, you are absolute miles away from being defendable. Big hitters (in my alliance) are comfortably zero’ing 13bn k40’s on the first hit.

So, if I were you: I would focus on building the strongest possible keep, but remain fully ghostable. This means far more higher level troops, build a kick ass pvp march in both ground and ranged, and learn to ghost fast af.

This way, you can visit enemy servers, play cat and mouse at temples etc, practice an effective portswap

By far the most fun Iv had playing this game was when I was ghostable.
I don't know what you are talking about. It is easier to get your wings with a trap keep.
Build about 400m t1 mounted. Main wall general gets all mounted flat buffs. Build many more t12 and t13 archers.All ach debuff gear on sub generals....etc
T1 defense is always fun

You are a lot closer to a PVP march than a trap keep. Just training T1s is not going to do it. You need at least 150M T1, deck out all your subs in Ach gear, flat refines on your wall general.

Start with a range march and then a ground march. Whichever way you go, make sure you max out that research!
Buffs before troops or your troops are only a points buffet
Stay ghostable - better a ghostable ninja attack then being hit with the heal bill of 2m troop layers and 1b t1 - ya that’s the number of t1 when you get up a bit further

Build a PvP archer march, keep your troops ghostable, push toward k36 and k37 and resist the urge to build a k35 t1 trap.  The k35 t1 traps are outdated now.  We have at least 6 k35 t1 traps f2p  that are useless in svs war because they used up their Source life reviving troops after getting zeroed a few times. (There are probably more too).
You shouldn’t have this many troops yet.  This is where people make huge mistakes.  K35 shouldn’t have 22 million troops.  Build up t14s, and work o your gear.

100%. I’m a fully ghostable k39. Got enough for k40 but holding back until I have enough for T15s too. 

I’ve pondered T1 or bulked out defence but it just doesn’t add up for me. Even as a strong k39/40, if you get sieged by a k44 you’re screwed. If you get rallied by 10+ players you’re screwed. The only thing it’s good against is someone slightly stronger soloing you or a small rally. Basically, players that don’t know what they’re doing. 

As it is, I can pick defences and drop ghost on opposing troop types and win most of the time (as long as their buffs aren’t ridiculous). If a huge whale hits me, I just stay ghosted. At worst I lose a march or maybe two if I pick a bad defence, not months of work. And it means I can happily unbubble and join rallies even when whales are floating around. There’s lots of 5-10Bn keeps on my server that can’t even unbubble during server war anymore as they’re just piñatas. 

Oh, and all that rss I would have wasted on troops to be killed can go to development, T15s and banging pvp marches. 

Unless you’re going to coin big, stay ghosting size.

Thank you both for your contribution and information, now I consult you, I stop training t1, I focus on my range and ground gait, what proportions should I have or am I going to directly improve myself to get the t14, I have approximately half a month since I reached k35, And what do I do with my other troops, somewhere I read that the t12 are better than the t13, is that true?
I wanted to try with the defense, but many talk about ghosts, with so many troops I couldn't cover them all, I would still receive the damage.

I’d definitely add t14 to your keep and then ghost as many high layer troops as you can leaving a layer of course. You will give out less points and do better in the power exchange. Thats how one of my alliance member plays and it does well for him.
T1s traps is not outdated but reviving T1s is a waste of SOLs. Know your limits and avoid getting zeroed! Test your limits in Battlefield first.
With PVP marches you need the bulk of your troops to be your top 3 layers.

On ranged march, people tend to go for a ratio close to 1:2:4:1 for T14-T11, so T12 is biggest layer. For ground, it’s more top heavy and like 4:3:2:1. There are loads of march calculators out there. Try Theria Games website for a simple one
Personally, I would now push to K36, then backfill since you will unlock Military Academy at K36.

Ignore everyone saying to build a defense unless you plan on training 1 billion t1 mounted. Seriously, don’t do it if you aren’t going all in. You will get to 200M or 300M or 500M t1’s then get zeroed by some k43-k45 or a quick rally. 

Unless you plan on spending thousands of dollars, you should focus on being a ghosting attack keep. Once you get to k40/k41/k42, then you can start thinking of building a defense. Until that time, you are nothing but a point piñata. 

Build 1 PvP march for each troop type then train enough t1 troops to fill your hospital for SvS.
https://preview.redd.it/e9l588ogwxud1.jpeg?width=1170&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=da6990eaef300cdfc604577c4b022fe49edb6be5
https://preview.redd.it/daagwd2iwxud1.jpeg?width=1170&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=4e47e59a7b5a4c91b13049c335a48001303f56f1
https://preview.redd.it/ogjucu5jwxud1.jpeg?width=1170&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=9e26d367096800b4c608fdc1090460fdbb475e25
https://preview.redd.it/0i3rmjekwxud1.jpeg?width=1170&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=8aa5cb02ac0d0b16e2b19e1c7fc7782fb0ba29e8

Here are how your PvP marches should be set up. At k35, your layers should be 1,000 troops. ONLY training enough troops for the PvP marches and t1’s to fill the hospital for SvS. Don’t train troops just to train troops. Focus on research and other buffs and growing your keep!
100% , we are on a server in the 1600s and our coiner is slaughtering people who think they're a t1 defence at k41 , it's a glorious sight ngl 🤣
(Only fill hospital if you spend enough for SOL , if you're low on SOL best to heal with rss) figured that's worth adding

When i get rallied by a 25Bn keep and \~20m troops, I win the power exchange 13x over

https://preview.redd.it/4vp9giq50zud1.jpeg?width=750&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=1ded9736d47ec56f405540adb68d79f0a3c36789
Lmao
^spot on. Unless you want to spend substantial coin! #coinstrike

submission.title: General effects
submission.body: Hello, I have a question about these buildings, market, forge, bunker, warehouse, embassy, ​​research factory, if I place a general and his buff or debuff does the castle get them either for defense or attack?

comments.body:
Only if it's the general for that specific building, and if the specialty says "When general is the xxx officer"

There are no tavern generals for the buildings you mentioned, only premium generals.

No, the buffs the generals give “while leading the army” or “while leading the army to attack” do not activate while they’re a duty general. 

there are generals that give special buffs while being duty generals, but they all say which building they go in.
I was wondering something along those lines also. If a general has a specialty that is a debuff it counts towards overall in city debuff if general is a duty officer correct?
From what I've read, it only counts if it specifies itself "as duty officer," or something like that. Normal debuffs won't work.
i think I'm going to try and see if I can get someone to scout me
Only applies if it's a duty officer and that's it. aND only the skills that say applied while a duty officer, others do nothing.

submission.title: Top debuff generals
submission.body: Hello, I would like your opinion on, the general would be easier to promote and with good benefits, Balduino I appears in many events, which other general would you say is essential to have on your team?

comments.body: andrew jakcson is good too..again it depends on which troop type you want debuff..only range debuff hojo , andrew and baldwin are good if overall than there are many generals.

I personally think Mark Anthony, Baldwin, Andrew Jackson are good.

Not all are easy to obtain. AJ is in the tavern last I saw.

This entirely depends on your keep! If your a low coiner or a trap AJ is a great option Baldwin is easy to get. Mark Anthony has got a lot easier. As your grow you need to focus on siege and ranged debuffs. Yan is one of the best cause of the siege HP debuff

I’ve spent a long time staring at spreadsheets on this. Top line: if you can’t get a premium Gen to 4 red star, you’re better off with a tavern Gen. I’d say a combo of 2/3 Jackson for the ranged debuff, 2x Cixi for overall defence, 2/3 Nero for all HP and then an arminius or Baldwin (more easy to ascend). That gives you a really solid spread of debuffs across troop types and buffs.

A 2 star Nero is 35% off all HP, and people get excited about gens with only siege HP. 

The other thing to flag is that while premium gens can end up with higher debuffs in one or two areas, they tend to be more focused. So you get more debuff on ranged attack for example, but lose out on little bits of everything else. That adds up and you end up with less rounded buffs.
Get 9 Jesters
🤣🤣 I bet there are only a handful that have him. Good thing he isn’t needed for General hall
It is better to ascend them or place several of them in the sub-cities, Oh and what do you mean by 2/3 (the amounts??)

As a F2P, go for combination and invest on general for long time. 5 star Nero / 5 star Andrew Jackson / 5 star Cixi / Meanwhile keep Baldwin in subcity to increase range debuff and later ascend him too.
I have 6 baldwin, is better to ascend him or put it on the 6 subcities

Depend if you are T1 mounted trap keep go for baldwin in 70% of your subcity, you might need good range debuff since 90% attack will be from Range rally Or Range solo unless it's K42+ where Mounted solo wrecks a T1 trap.
He means to have 2-3 of each as sub gens

Hi, I keep seeing the word *trap* used. Can you explain it in this context please. I've been playing nearly 2 years and still know very little. I'm going to have a shock whenever I get to start pvp. I'm only k32. Probably due to forgetting to bubble during svs. 3 times!! Ho hum.

Yes, sorry for confusion. 2 or 3 - depends how many sub spots you have.

On the question of ascending one or putting copies into more subs, at the start, you’re best off filling your subs with decent gens rather than  trying to ascend one. This can include premium gens as their buffs at no ascension are usually better than a tavern Gen, eg no star Hojo or Mark Anthony better than no star Jackson. 

It only makes sense to shift to all tavern gens once you’ve got the ascending going (probably around 3 star). 

So in my case, I had 2 Nero, 2 Cixi, 2 Jackson, 2 Anthony and 1 Hojo when I had no sub gens ascended. Then as I got copies and ascended the tavern gens, I phased out the premium gens.
Never been solod by a k43 or higher mounted.
You might not get solo but they make an impact especially with the large march size.

I have 7 Cimons, 3 Baldwins and a Barbarian King.... 

I like range debuff 😂
It depends what your other sub gens are. You want them all to have original buffs 20%+. As you start getting better sub gens you can consolidate.

Traps are lower in level and smallish in power. The point is to bait someone into hitting you without a scout. The build includes an excessive amount of T1 troops (100m-250m at the very minimum if you don't face monster whales) and only defensive layers for the rest of the troops (in the 10ks or so). Then you need to put all of the debuffs you can stack on your wall and subs and refine wall gear/dragon for the whole number values that match your chosen T1 troop.

Thanks, you explained that very clearly.  I read about making up layers of different troops. I did that before the undead attack at the end of svs. I only needed to heal 2 times because I trained those accending layers of troops. Plus I'd spent a heap of gold and speed ups in the acedemy in medical and defence. The game takes enough time and I've only got a k32 and a k28 farm. If/when I start thinking about pvp, I'll be here 27/7.
